title: nie-daemon E2E: two daemons exchange a message via relay
description:
**What:** End-to-end test verifying that Alice sends a chat message via her daemon's REST API and Bob receives it via his daemon's WebSocket /ws/events — exercising the full stack: browser API → nie-daemon → relay → nie-daemon → WebSocket.

**Algorithm:**
1. Add to daemon/tests/e2e.rs (requires --ignored tag, two daemon instances + relay):
2. Test: test_two_daemon_message_exchange:
   a. Spawn relay on random port (reuse spawn_relay from relay tests)
   b. Generate two identities (Alice, Bob) in tmp dirs
   c. Start Alice daemon on port A connecting to relay
   d. Start Bob daemon on port B connecting to relay
   e. Wait until both daemons report relay connected (poll /health + /api/users for each other, timeout 10s)
   f. Bob connects WS /ws/events with Bob's token
   g. Alice: POST /api/send { text: 'hello from alice' } with Alice's token
   h. Bob's WS: await next event, timeout 10s
   i. Assert: event.type == 'message_received', event.text == 'hello from alice'
   j. Assert: event.from == Alice's pub_id (64 hex chars)
   k. Cleanup: stop both daemons, relay

**Defensive programming rules:**
- All waits use tokio::time::timeout — never infinite
- 10s timeout on relay sync (step e), 10s timeout on WS event (step h)
- Cleanup runs even on test failure (use Drop guard)
- Assert pub_id structural format: length 64, all hex chars

**Agent team:** Implementer (reads relay/tests/stress.rs for spawn_relay pattern) + Validator.

**Tests:** This is the E2E test. Oracle: known message string 'hello from alice' is the expected value sent by Alice; Bob receives it unmodified. Independent oracle confirmed by round-trip through relay (two separate processes).

Run with: cargo test -p nie-daemon e2e -- --ignored
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-daemon e2e -- --ignored passes within 30s; event.text matches exactly; cargo clippy -- -D warnings exits 0
close_reason: E2E test implemented: daemon/tests/e2e.rs — two daemon instances exchange via relay, passes in <1s
